Title: A de novo Gene Promotes Seed Germination Under Drought Stress in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: de novo small protein; ~8 kDa; alpha helix plus disordered sequence
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Mainly nucleus; also cytoplasm and cell membrane
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in dry seed, imbibing seed, and silique
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in dry seed, imbibing seed, and silique
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lower hydrogen peroxide supports osmotic-stress germination; folate supports one-carbon metabolism.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 400 mM mannitol osmotic stress; exogenous ABA
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; RT-qPCR; western blot; subcellular localization; RNA-seq; hydrogen peroxide measurement; yeast two-hybrid
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes seed germination under osmotic stress, is associated with lower hydrogen peroxide accumulation, and is integrated into folate biosynthesis-related regulation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among seven tested de novo genes, SWK was the only one with a clear phenotype, promoting seed germination under 400 mM mannitol stress. 2. T-DNA and CRISPR knockout mutants showed reduced germination under osmotic stress, whereas overexpression lines showed higher germination. 3. SWK encodes a fully translated ~8 kDa protein mainly localized in the nucleus. 4. cytHPPK/DHPS positively regulates SWK expression upstream. 5. SWK positively affects GSTF9 expression and lowers hydrogen peroxide content under stress. 6. SWK knockout reduced DHFS expression, indicating integration into the folate biosynthesis pathway.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Under exogenous ABA stress, mutants had higher germination than an overexpression line, suggesting SWK may increase ABA sensitivity during germination.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene to improve drought/osmotic-stress seed germination in breeding or engineering; may be useful in climate-resilience strategies, with attention to ABA-related germination responses.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used for overexpression/localization constructs
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AT1G69190/cytHPPK-DHPS (no direct interaction detected); GSTF9 (no direct interaction detected); DHFS (no direct interaction detected)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A de novo Arabidopsis-specific gene likely originated from noncoding sequence and later integrated into extant stress/redox and folate-related pathways. Presence in natural populations is associated with lower precipitation of driest quarter in African accessions, suggesting possible drought adaptation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Leaf RNA-seq for eGWAS in 414 accessions; imbibing-seed RNA-seq under control and 400 mM mannitol, GSA: CRA014531

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced under mannitol stress in WT/OE relative to swk mutant
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced under mannitol stress in WT/OE relative to swk mutant
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Detoxification of hydrogen peroxide contributes to osmotic-stress resistance and seed germination.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq candidate identification; RT-qPCR; hydrogen peroxide measurement in genotypes affecting upstream genes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts downstream of SWK and cytHPPK/DHPS; higher GSTF9 expression is associated with lower hydrogen peroxide and improved germination under osmotic stress.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSTF9 was identified as a candidate downstream gene of SWK from RNA-seq. 2. Under mannitol stress, GSTF9 expression was highest in SWK overexpression lines, intermediate in WT, and lowest in swk mutants. 3. Genotypes with lower GSTF9 expression showed higher hydrogen peroxide accumulation. 4. In the cytHPPK/DHPS mutant, GSTF9 expression followed the same reduced pattern as SWK, placing GSTF9 downstream in the pathway.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream marker or co-engineering target for improving seed germination under osmotic/drought stress via redox detoxification.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SWK (no direct interaction detected by yeast two-hybrid)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper cites prior evidence that GSTF9 increases mannitol resistance by degrading hydrogen peroxide; in this study it is validated as a downstream node rather than the primary discovery target.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of imbibing seeds under control and 400 mM mannitol, GSA: CRA014531

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 400 mM mannitol osmotic stress context for SWK pathway study
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: eGWAS candidate evaluation; knockdown test for SWK expression; RT-qPCR in SWK knockout vs WT
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions downstream of SWK within folate biosynthesis-related regulation; DHFS expression decreases when SWK is knocked out.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DHFS showed the strongest eGWAS signal associated with SWK expression but DHFS knockdown did not alter SWK expression, arguing against it being an upstream regulator. 2. DHFS expression was significantly lower in SWK knockout lines than in WT. 3. These data support DHFS as a downstream folate-pathway gene influenced by SWK.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SWK (no direct interaction detected by yeast two-hybrid)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes downstream genes after DHFS did not change, possibly due to feedback inhibition in folate metabolism.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: eGWAS using 414-accession expression data; RT-qPCR in SWK knockout lines

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Folate supports one-carbon metabolism; reduced hydrogen peroxide supports osmotic/drought stress tolerance and seed germination.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis; RT-qPCR of SWK and GSTF9 expression; seed germination assay; hydrogen peroxide measurement
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes seed germination under mannitol stress and positively regulates SWK and GSTF9 expression while reducing hydrogen peroxide accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. eGWAS and mutant analysis identified cytHPPK/DHPS as an upstream positive regulator linked to SWK expression. 2. cytHPPK/DHPS mutants showed reduced seed germination under 400 mM mannitol stress. 3. SWK expression was significantly reduced in the cytHPPK/DHPS mutant background. 4. GSTF9 expression and hydrogen peroxide levels in the cytHPPK/DHPS mutant supported a pathway in which cytHPPK/DHPS promotes stress tolerance via SWK/GSTF9 and folate-related processes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target for improving drought/osmotic-stress germination, especially together with SWK/GSTF9-related modules; caution that broader folate-pathway effects may occur.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SWK (no direct interaction detected by yeast two-hybrid)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as an Arabidopsis-specific duplicate of mitHPPK/DHPS; prior work cited indicates it affects folate content. In this paper it is functionally treated as an upstream core component connected to nutrient metabolism and redox status.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: eGWAS using leaf RNA-seq from 414 accessions; RNA-seq database screening; seed RNA-seq GSA: CRA014531
